claimText: In India, snow leopards are found in the mountainous regions of Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Sikkim, and Arunachal Pradesh.
evidenceExcerpt: Found in mountainous regions of J&K, Ladakh, HP, Uttarakhand, Sikkim, and Arunachal Pradesh.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical distribution of species in India.
distractorHooks: Including non-Himalayan or northeastern states like Meghalaya, Assam, or Western Ghats states.
Stating they are exclusively found in Ladakh.

claimText: Hemis National Park, Kargil, and Leh have the highest extensive densities of snow leopards globally.
evidenceExcerpt: Hemis National Park, Kargil, and Leh have the highest extensive densities globally.
examUse: Statement-based or correct-pair question linking species to specific protected areas.
distractorHooks: Naming other high-altitude national parks like Namdapha or Khangchendzonga as having the highest density.
Claiming the highest density is in Central Asian countries outside India.

claimText: Snow leopards are solitary animals and, unlike other big cats, they cannot roar.
evidenceExcerpt: Unlike other big cats, snow leopards cannot roar.
examUse: Statement-based question on the biological and behavioral characteristics of big cats.
distractorHooks: Stating that snow leopards live in prides or groups.
Claiming that snow leopards can roar like tigers and lions.

claimText: The snow leopard is listed as Vulnerable (VU) on the IUCN Red List, and is protected under WPA Schedule I, CITES Appendix I, and CMS Appendix I.
evidenceExcerpt: IUCN Status is Vulnerable (VU); listed under WPA Schedule I, CITES Appendix I, and CMS Appendix I.
examUse: Match-the-following or statement-based question on the conservation status of flagship species.
distractorHooks: Endangered or Critically Endangered IUCN status.
WPA Schedule II or CITES Appendix II.

claimText: The Bishkek Declaration (2017) and Samarkand Resolution (2024) are international initiatives focused on snow leopard conservation.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation initiatives include the Bishkek Declaration (2017), Samarkand Resolution (2024)
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on international environmental declarations and their associated species.
distractorHooks: Associating the Bishkek Declaration or Samarkand Resolution with tiger, rhino, or elephant conservation.
Confusing them with the St. Petersburg Declaration (tigers).

claimText: CaTRAT (Camera Trap data Repository and Analysis Tool) is utilized to identify and track Himalayan snow leopards.
evidenceExcerpt: CaTRAT to identify Himalayan snow leopards.
examUse: Statement-based question on technology used in wildlife conservation.
distractorHooks: Associating CaTRAT with marine species tracking.
Claiming CaTRAT is a satellite-based forest fire monitoring tool.
